dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI had the strongest day because Codex earned explicit praise for transparency, open-sourcing, and team responsiveness, while its ChatGPT scaling post drew engaged technical discussion instead of backlash [E1][E2].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI won on both tone and coverage: it had two direct front-page stories, one clearly favorable around Codex and one broadly constructive around ChatGPT infrastructure. That gave it the most substantial positive surface area of any tracked provider on the day [E1][E2].

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex got praise for being open source and for strong communication from the team, and the ChatGPT/PostgreSQL writeup sparked interested technical discussion with only limited skepticism about architecture choices [E1][E2].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Comments on the corn experiment argued Claude was being oversold as autonomous, and Claude Code was singled out for regressions and undocumented changes in a broader discussion of agentic coding tools [E3][E4].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini's Nano Banana was cited as producing cluttered, hard-to-read diagrams with garbled text, which was the clearest Gemini-specific reaction in the snapshot [E5].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: Copilot only appeared as a comparison point, but one commenter explicitly held up its checkpoint feature as something Codex should copy [E6].

evidence.summary: OpenAI's 'Unrolling the Codex agent loop' drew praise for making Codex CLI internals open source and for strong maintainer communication in GitHub issues and PRs.

'Scaling PostgreSQL to power 800M ChatGPT users' prompted interested technical discussion and curiosity about OpenAI engineering, with some skepticism about offloading new features to other databases.
In 'Proof of Corn,' the main Anthropic-specific reaction was that Claude was being oversold: commenters argued a human was still doing the real research and coordination.
In the Gas Town discussion, Claude Code was cited as having an unusually high number of regressions or undocumented changes, reinforcing criticism of current AI coding tools.
The Gas Town thread criticized Gemini's Nano Banana diagrams as cluttered, hard to decipher, and full of garbled text and inconsistent visuals.
In the Codex thread, a commenter said they wanted checkpoints 'ala Copilot,' using Copilot's checkpoint feature as the favorable reference point.
